title: Gangtok district holds monsoon preparedness meeting - Sikkim Express
article_text:
Wednesday, May 20, 2026 08:15 [IST] Last Update: Wednesday, May 20, 2026 02:45 [IST]





Gangtok district holds monsoon preparedness meeting

GANGTOK, (IPR): The district-level monsoon preparedness meeting was conducted Tuesday by the District Disaster Management Authority (DDMA)-Gangtok at the conference hall of Zilla Bhawan, Gangtok. The meeting was chaired by District Collector-cum-Chairman, DDMA-Gangtok, Rohan Agawane.

In his keynote address, District Collector-cum-Chairman, DDMA-Gangtok, Rohan Agawane, highlighted that poor drainage systems and mismanagement of water are among the root causes of environmental damage in hilly terrains. He noted that due to climate change, the intensity of rainfall over short periods has increased, affecting drainage systems and water collection points, as these systems are often not designed to handle such intensity. He therefore underscored the need to prioritise resilient infrastructure development.

He also stressed the importance of effective water management, noting that most of the main urban areas of Gangtok are built along steep mountain ridges and hillsides rather than on flat valley floors, making them highly vulnerable to disasters and underscoring the need for robust disaster management measures. He stated that special focus would be placed on road drains, jhoras, and PHE pipelines that often obstruct drainage channels.

He also emphasised Section 30 of the Disaster Management Act, 2005, which empowers the DDMA to issue directions to line departments involved in risk management, with non-compliance potentially attracting provisions under Section 51 of the Act, which pertains to penalties for obstruction or non-compliance with such directions.

He further informed that a Disaster Management Team would be constituted, headed by a Disaster Management Coordinator, followed by Disaster Management Supervisors and Disaster Management Assistants, to map the entire district and identify vulnerable points within their respective jurisdictions. He concluded by stating that an inventory of all machinery, equipment, and assets related to disaster management within the district would also be prepared.

Discover more Newspaper Newspapers

Superintendent of Police, Gangtok District, Mahendra Subba, in his brief address, requested active coordination and cooperation from all line departments during emergencies and natural calamities. He highlighted the need for responsible frontline personnel and stressed the importance of ensuring compliance during such situations. He also emphasised the importance of quick decision-making during emergencies and urged all stakeholders to remain in a state of constant preparedness, noting that natural calamities are unpredictable and can occur at any time.

Zilla Adhyaksha, Gangtok District, Balaram Adhikari, reiterated the importance of being ever-ready to respond to emergencies. He also emphasised effective inter-departmental coordination for the smooth execution of disaster management activities. He requested the clearance of debris from drainage systems to prevent waterlogging and also stressed the provision of relief materials and preparedness of relief camps.

Officials from line departments, namely the Roads and Bridges department (NH Wing), Gangtok District, NHIDCL, GREF 129 RCC, GREF 87 RCC, Forest and Environment department, Power Department, Food and Civil Supplies department, STNM Hospital, Central Referral Hospital, Urban Development department, Gangtok Municipal Corporation, Public Health Engineering department, and Water Resources and River Development department, also presented their monsoon preparedness plans. The presentations covered action plans and standard operating procedures (SOPs) for dealing with monsoon-related disasters, along with challenges faced at various sites.

Emphasis was also placed on the identification of vulnerable locations, preventive measures undertaken, and deployment of manpower, machinery, and materials for mitigation. The status of ongoing works was also presented, along with emergency contact details of officials designated for coordination during emergencies.

Earlier, the welcome address was delivered by the joint director, DDMA, Sonam Wongyal Lepcha, while the vote of thanks was proposed by SDM, Gangtok, Mahendra Chettri.

The meeting was also attended by officials from various line departments and stakeholders.

title: Storm causes damage to 168 structures in Mizoram - The News Mill
article_text:
A spell of intense pre-monsoon weather has battered Mizoram over the past three days, damaging property and infrastructure across multiple districts, officials said on Wednesday. No loss of life has been reported.

Discover more weather weather conditions Local News

According to the State Emergency Operation Centre under the Disaster Management and Rehabilitation department, at least 168 structures have been destroyed or partially damaged since Monday. These include 155 private buildings and 13 government, community and church establishments.

Lunglei district was the worst affected, with 85 private houses damaged or flattened. Three Baptist church buildings also sustained significant damage.

In Aizawl district, 44 houses were damaged, while 14 homes were destroyed in Siaha district, which borders Myanmar. In total, eight districts reported damage.

Services hit, connectivity disrupted

Discover more Weather Newspapers Policy Briefs

High-velocity winds uprooted electric poles in several locations, leading to power outages, including in parts of the state capital. Road connectivity was disrupted by landslides and rockfalls, making several stretches inaccessible.

Mobile and internet services were also affected due to technical faults triggered by the weather conditions, hampering communication in some areas.

Relief operations underway

District administrations, assisted by the State Disaster Response Force, police and local volunteers, have been mobilised for relief and restoration work.

Officials said immediate assistance includes financial aid, tarpaulins for temporary shelter, and rehabilitation support for families whose homes were destroyed. Damage assessment is ongoing, with authorities urging residents to remain alert amid continuing pre-monsoon weather conditions.

title: Southwest Monsoon Gains Strength: Rain Likely Across Andhra Pradesh And Telangana; Thunderstorms, Gusty Winds - NativePlanet
article_text:
The southwest monsoon is gradually strengthening over southern India, with weather activity expected to intensify across Andhra Pradesh and Telangana over the coming days. Meteorological conditions are showing signs of increased moisture movement, cloud development, and thunderstorm activity, bringing the possibility of scattered rainfall and gusty winds across several regions.
Weather authorities have indicated that pre-monsoon activity is becoming more active even before the official onset of the southwest monsoon over mainland India.
According to weather observations, southwest monsoon winds have already advanced into parts of the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and adjoining southern regions, with conditions becoming favourable for further progress toward coastal Andhra Pradesh.
Meteorologists say changing atmospheric patterns and upper-air circulations are helping create cloud formations across southern states, increasing the possibility of rainfall activity during the next few days.
The weather shift comes after several regions experienced persistent heat conditions through the past few weeks.
Light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds is expected in parts of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana between May 20 and May 22, 2026.
Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Rayalaseema and interior Telangana regions may witness scattered showers, especially during afternoon and evening hours.
Some isolated areas could also witness stronger thunderstorms with wind speeds potentially reaching up to 60 km/h, according to weather assessments.
Hyderabad and nearby districts may experience changing weather conditions through the evening, with sudden cloud build-up and brief rain spells remaining possible.
Despite rain activity, heat conditions are not expected to disappear completely.
Northern parts of Telangana could continue witnessing temperatures in the 40°C-42°C range, while sections of coastal Andhra Pradesh may remain hot during daytime hours before rain activity develops later.
The combination of heat and humidity may also increase discomfort levels in several areas.
Weather experts have advised residents to remain cautious during evening hours because of the possibility of:
Fishermen have also been advised to exercise caution due to strong wind activity over parts of the Bay of Bengal.
The increasing cloud activity over southern India indicates that weather systems are becoming more organised ahead of the broader southwest monsoon progression.
While rainfall may remain scattered initially, meteorologists believe the coming weeks could bring stronger monsoon-related developments across the Telugu states, with isolated heavy rainfall events remaining a possibility.

title: High moisture content fueling thunderstorms in state: IMD - Deccan Herald
article_text:
Motorists wade though a flooded road in Hubballi.
Hubballi: A combination of high temperatures and abundant atmospheric moisture is triggering intense thunderstorm activity in the state.
According to N Puviarasan, Head of the India Meteorological Department (IMD) Bengaluru Centre, the current weather pattern is favourable for the formation of widespread thunderstorms accompanied by heavy rain, lightning and gusty winds.
He says the intense showers are primarily due to the high moisture content available, combined with rising daytime temperatures across the state.
“The temperature over interior Karnataka (both north and south) has remained significantly high during the daytime. At the same time, substantial moisture is incursion from the Arabian Sea and the Bay of Bengal. This combination is creating atmospheric instability, leading to intense thunderstorm activity and heavy pre-monsoon showers,” he said.
He explained that the sharp heating of land surfaces during summer causes warm air to rise rapidly. When moisture-laden winds enter the region, resulting in sudden evening showers and thunderstorms.
Puviarasan said that a one-degree increase in temperature leads to a 7 mm increase in moisture content in the air, resulting in a higher quantity of pre-monsoon showers.
Meteorologists say such conditions are typical during the pre-monsoon season, but this year the rainfall intensity has been higher in coastal and north interior Karnataka.
Copious pre-monsoon showers have reduced the rainfall deficit in Karnataka.
As of May 19, the overall state average rainfall stood at a 2% deficit, an improvement from the 11% deficit a week ago. Against the normal pre-monsoon showers of 85 mm rainfall, the coastal districts received 110 mm of rainfall.
North interior Karnataka received 67 mm of rainfall against a normal of 54 mm. South interior Karnataka usually receives 102 mm of rainfall in a normal year; however, in April-May, it received only 71 mm. The Malnad region received only 106 mm instead of the average of 119 mm of rainfall.
Puviarasan said the state could witness such highly intense thundershowers in the north for the next two days before subduing across the state.

title: India Weather Update: दिल्ली-यूपी में 25 मई तक हीटवेव का ‘ऑरेंज अलर्ट’, केरल में इस दिन दस्तक देगा मॉनसून - Rashtriya Khabar
article_text:
नई दिल्ली: देशभर में इन दिनों मौसम के अलग-अलग और विपरीत रंग देखने को मिल रहे हैं। कहीं लोगों को आसमान से बरसती आग और भीषण गर्मी का सामना करना पड़ रहा है, तो कहीं मूसलाधार बारिश ने मौसम को बेहद सुहावना बना दिया है। देश की राजधानी दिल्ली समेत उत्तर और मध्य भारत के अधिकांश राज्यों में चिलचिलाती धूप और लू से बुरा हाल है, जबकि दूसरी ओर पूर्वोत्तर और दक्षिण भारत के राज्यों में प्री-मॉनसून बारिश का दौर लगातार जारी है। भारतीय मौसम विज्ञान विभाग (IMD) ने स्थिति की गंभीरता को देखते हुए 20 से 25 मई तक दिल्ली-एनसीआर में हीटवेव (Heatwave) का ‘ऑरेंज अलर्ट’ जारी कर दिया है।
आईएमडी के ताजा बुलेटिन के मुताबिक, बुधवार को दिल्ली का अधिकतम तापमान 45 डिग्री सेल्सियस के रिकॉर्ड स्तर तक पहुंचने का अनुमान है, जबकि न्यूनतम तापमान 27 डिग्री सेल्सियस के आसपास रह सकता है। तेज झुलसाने वाली धूप और थपेड़े मारती गर्म हवाओं के कारण दोपहर के समय लोगों का घरों से बाहर निकलना दूभर हो गया है। दिल्ली के अलावा पंजाब, हरियाणा, चंडीगढ़ और उत्तर प्रदेश में भी इन गर्म शुष्क हवाओं का व्यापक असर देखने को मिलेगा। मौसम विभाग के अनुसार, इन राज्यों में 20 से 25 मई के दौरान 20 से 30 किलोमीटर प्रति घंटे की तेज रफ्तार से धूलभरी गर्म हवाएं (लू) चलने की आशंका है।
एक तरफ जहां उत्तर भारत तप रहा है, वहीं दूसरी ओर दक्षिण-पश्चिम मॉनसून (South-West Monsoon) तेजी से आगे बढ़ रहा है। अगले तीन से चार दिनों के दौरान दक्षिण-पूर्वी अरब सागर, कोमोरिन क्षेत्र, बंगाल की खाड़ी और अंडमान सागर के शेष हिस्सों में मॉनसून के आगे बढ़ने के लिए मौसमी परिस्थितियां पूरी तरह अनुकूल बनी हुई हैं। केरल में मॉनसून के 26 मई के आसपास दस्तक देने की प्रबल संभावना जताई गई है। इसके साथ ही, पूर्वोत्तर के राज्यों असम और मेघालय में 21 से 25 मई तक भारी से अत्यंत भारी बारिश का ‘रेड और ऑरेंज अलर्ट’ जारी किया गया है। वहीं अरुणाचल प्रदेश, नागालैंड, मणिपुर, मिजोरम और त्रिपुरा में भी 22 से 25 मई के दौरान मूसलाधार बारिश होने की चेतावनी दी गई है।
पूर्वी भारत के राज्यों के लिए राहत की खबर है। 20 मई से बिहार, झारखंड और पश्चिम बंगाल के कई इलाकों में आंधी-तूफान के साथ हल्की से मध्यम बारिश का अनुमान है, जिससे तापमान में गिरावट आएगी। वहीं ओडिशा में मंगलवार को 21 स्थानों पर अधिकतम तापमान 40 डिग्री सेल्सियस से ऊपर दर्ज किया गया, जहां झारसुगुड़ा 44.8 डिग्री सेल्सियस के साथ राज्य का सबसे गर्म स्थान रहा। दूसरी तरफ, मरुधरा यानी राजस्थान के चित्तौड़गढ़ में अधिकतम तापमान 46.3 डिग्री सेल्सियस दर्ज किया गया। श्रीगंगानगर, अलवर, पिलानी और राजधानी जयपुर समेत कई शहर भी भीषण गर्मी की चपेट में हैं, जहां अगले एक सप्ताह तक लू का कहर जारी रहेगा।
पश्चिम और मध्य भारत की बात करें तो महाराष्ट्र के विदर्भ क्षेत्र में गर्मी से बुरा हाल है। अमरावती में तापमान 46.8 डिग्री और वर्धा में 46.5 डिग्री सेल्सियस दर्ज किया गया है, जिसके चलते आईएमडी ने कई जिलों के लिए ऑरेंज अलर्ट जारी किया है। इसके अलावा, अमूमन ठंडे रहने वाले पहाड़ी राज्य उत्तराखंड के मैदानी इलाकों में भी तापमान 40 डिग्री के पार पहुंचने की आशंका को देखते हुए शासन-प्रशासन को अलर्ट रहने के निर्देश दिए गए हैं। देहरादून मौसम केंद्र के पूर्वानुमान के मुताबिक, आगामी दिनों में हरिद्वार, ऊधम सिंह नगर और देहरादून के मैदानी क्षेत्रों में गर्मी का असर सबसे ज्यादा रहने की आशंका है, जिससे निपटने के लिए जिलाधिकारियों को आवश्यक तैयारियां करने को कहा गया है।

title: ഇരട്ട ന്യൂനമർദ്ദ പാത്തി, സംസ്ഥാനത്ത് ഇന്നും നാളെയും കനത്ത മഴ, ജാഗ്രതാ നിർദ്ദേശം - Karma News - Voice of common people
article_text:
തിരുവനന്തപുരം: രാജ്യത്ത് രൂപപ്പെട്ട ഇരട്ട ന്യൂനമർദ്ദ പാത്തികളുടെ പ്രഭാവത്തിൽ സംസ്ഥാനത്ത് ഇന്നും നാളെയും ഒറ്റപ്പെട്ടയിടങ്ങളിൽ ശക്തമായ മഴയ്ക്ക് സാധ്യതയെന്ന് കേന്ദ്ര കാലാവസ്ഥാ വകുപ്പിന്റെ മുന്നറിയിപ്പ്. കനത്ത മഴയ്‌ക്കൊപ്പം ശക്തമായ ഇടിമിന്നലിനും മണിക്കൂറിൽ 40 മുതൽ 50 കിലോമീറ്റർ വരെ വേഗതയിൽ വീശിയേക്കാവുന്ന കാറ്റിനും സാധ്യതയുണ്ട്. പൊതുജനങ്ങൾ അതീവ ജാഗ്രത പാലിക്കണമെന്ന് അധികൃതർ നിർദ്ദേശിച്ചു.
കാലവർഷം മേയ് 26-ഓടെ (നാല് ദിവസം മുന്നോട്ടോ പിന്നോട്ടോ വ്യത്യാസം വരാം) കേരളത്തിൽ എത്തിയേക്കുമെന്നാണ് പ്രവചനം. ഇതിന്റെ മുന്നോടിയായുള്ള അന്തരീക്ഷ വ്യതിയാനങ്ങളാണ് ഇപ്പോൾ സംസ്ഥാനത്ത് മഴ ശക്തമാക്കാൻ കാരണം.
ശക്തമായ മഴയുടെ പശ്ചാത്തലത്തിൽ വിവിധ ജില്ലകളിൽ കാലാവസ്ഥാ വകുപ്പ് യെല്ലോ അലർട്ട് പ്രഖ്യാപിച്ചു. ഇന്ന് (മേയ് 20): ആലപ്പുഴ, എറണാകുളം, തൃശൂർ, മലപ്പുറം ജില്ലകളിൽ യെല്ലോ അലർട്ടായിരിക്കും. നാളെ (മേയ് 21): തിരുവനന്തപുരം, കൊല്ലം, പത്തനംതിട്ട, ആലപ്പുഴ, കോട്ടയം, ഇടുക്കി, എറണാകുളം എന്നീ തെക്കൻ-മധ്യ കേരളത്തിലെ ഏഴ് ജില്ലകളിൽ ഒരൊറ്റപ്പെട്ടയിടങ്ങളിൽ ശക്തമായ മഴയ്ക്ക് സാധ്യതയുണ്ട്.
24 മണിക്കൂറിൽ 64.5 മില്ലിമീറ്റർ മുതൽ 115.5 മില്ലിമീറ്റർ വരെ മഴ ലഭിക്കുമെന്നാണ് കണക്കുകൂട്ടൽ. നിലവിൽ ദക്ഷിണേന്ത്യൻ അന്തരീക്ഷത്തെ സ്വാധീനിക്കുന്നത് രണ്ട് വലിയ ന്യൂനമർദ്ദ പാത്തികളാണ്: കിഴക്കൻ ഉത്തർപ്രദേശിൽ നിന്ന് തുടങ്ങി മധ്യ മഹാരാഷ്ട്ര, കർണാടക വഴി കേരളത്തിന് മുകളിലൂടെ ലക്ഷദ്വീപ് വരെ നീളുന്ന ഒന്നാമത്തെ പാത്തി. തെക്കൻ ലക്ഷദ്വീപിനോട് ചേർന്നുള്ള അറബിക്കടലിൽ നിന്ന് തുടങ്ങി കേരളം വഴി ആന്ധ്രാപ്രദേശിന് മുകളിലുള്ള ചക്രവാതചുഴി വരെ നീളുന്ന രണ്ടാമത്തെ പാത്തി.
ഇവയുടെ സംയുക്ത സ്വാധീനത്തിലാണ് കാറ്റും മഴയും ശക്തമാകുന്നത്. കനത്ത കാറ്റിന് സാധ്യതയുള്ളതിനാൽ തീരദേശവാസികളും മലയോര മേഖലയിലുള്ളവരും മുൻകരുതലുകൾ സ്വീകരിക്കണം.

other_info.csv_title: ഇരട്ട ന്യൂനമർദ്ദ പാത്തി, സംസ്ഥാനത്ത് ഇന്നും നാളെയും കനത്ത മഴ, ജാഗ്രതാ നിർദ്ദേശം - Karma News - Voice of common people
